summary: Recent updates to the 2D graphics component of Firefox focus on enhancing font handling and optimizing performance. The browser now ensures that the default font family is properly initialized before usage, which helps avoid issues with displaying text. Additionally, there are improvements to the efficiency of processing layout metrics in text frames, which can lead to smoother performance for rendering web pages. **Moreover, the allowed features for video processing have been refined, shifting to a blocklist approach for unsupported features, ensuring that only compatible elements are utilized, which ultimately aims to provide a more reliable video playback experience.** These changes collectively contribute to better user experience and stability, particularly in text rendering and media handling.
